docs.title: Welcome to the PCM Documentation Project
docs.text:
Creating better PCM documentation, one function at a time.

Who is this for? CMM Programmers, Automation Enthusiasts, and Metrology Engineers looking to master the Calypso scripting language.

docs.title: What is PCM?
docs.text:
PCM (Parameter Coded Measurement) is the internal scripting engine used within the Zeiss Calypso software. While Calypso handles the heavy lifting of CMM (Coordinate Measuring Machine) data collection, PCM is used to control the logic, process data, and customize inspection routines.

Whether you are writing a simple script to check tolerances or a complex automation script, this documentation aims to make PCM accessible to everyone.

docs.title: Why use this site?
docs.text:
• For Operators: Clear, simple examples that help you automate repetitive checks.
  • For Engineers: A reference guide for the internal syntax and math functions.
  • For Programmers: An open resource for extending Calypso's capabilities.

docs.title: Get Involved
docs.text: Documentation is never truly finished. If you find a function that works differently than documented, or if you have a snippet that helps your workflow, we want to know.

docs.title: Arithmetic
docs.text: Operator Result + Addition - Subtraction * Multiplication / Division ** Exponential

docs.title: Arithmetic Examples
docs.text:
foo = 2
bar = 5

// Addition -- of numbers AND concatenation of strings
display(foo + bar) // 7
display("Hello" + "Goodbye") // HelloGoodbye

// Subtraction
display(foo - bar) // -3

// Multiplication
display(foo * bar) // 10

// Division
display(foo / bar) // 0.400000

// Exponential
display(foo ** bar) // 2^5, which is 32

docs.title: Boolean Comparison
docs.text: Operator Result < Less Than > Greater Than <> Not Equal To == Equal To >= Greater Than or Equal To <= Less Than or Equal To

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All return the Boolean values (true or false)
  • Can compare Numbers and Strings
  • Can compare Booleans with Equal To (==) or Not Equal To (<>)

docs.title: Boolean Comparison Examples
docs.text:
foo = 2
bar = 5

// Number Examples
display(foo < bar) // true
display(foo > bar) // false
display(foo <> foo) // false
display(foo == foo) // true
display(bar >= foo) // true
display(bar <= bar) // true

// String Examples
display("foobar" == "baz") // false
display("foobar" <> "") // true

// Numbers & Strings do not combine, be careful
display(0 == "0") // false
display("0" == 0) // false

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description name type optional desc ... Any Yes Remaining arguments

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Converts a given number from radians to degrees.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Integer, Double No number to convert from radians to degrees

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: Integer, Double - degree equivalent of the given value from radians

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Converts a given number from degrees to radians.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Integer, Double No number to convert from degrees to radians

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: Integer, Double - radian equivalent of the given value from degrees

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Unofficial command to execute SmallTalk code through PCM scripting.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description commands String No SmallTalk code to execute ... Any Yes Remaining arguments.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • a practical joke by Eric Moberg on the Zeiss Forums

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Provides the ASCII code from given character string

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description character String No Character string from which to derive ASCII code

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Seems to relate to ANSI codes when getting into extended characters…
  • The inverse of chr

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Provides the character string from given ASCII code

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Number No ASCII code from which to derive character string

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: String - the character string from given ASCII code number

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Seems to relate to ANSI codes when getting into extended characters…
  • The inverse of asc

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
msg = "Rotate by 90" + chr(176)
display(msg) // Rotate by 90°

tab = chr(9)
display("Hello" + tab + "World!") // Hello        World!F

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • A reliable way to get the correct carriage return & new line characters without using chr, particularly for Windows

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
display("First Line", cr(), "Second Line")
// First Line
// Second Line

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Number No Integer or Double to convert to a string

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • when number is a Double, there will be a trailing "d" character in the resultant string

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Number No The number to be converted to left-justified string totalCharacters Number Yes Total number of characters of the final left-justified string decimalPlaces Number Yes Total number of decimal places to display

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: String - rounded, left-justified string of the given number

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• if totalCharacters and decimalPlaces are not provided, default is 4 decimal places
  • the decimal point . is not considered for totalCharacters

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Number No The number to be converted to right-justified string totalCharacters Number Yes Total number of characters of the final right-justified string decimalPlaces Number Yes Total number of decimal places to display

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: String - rounded, right-justified string of the given number

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• if totalCharacters and decimalPlaces are not provided, default is 4 decimal places
  • the decimal point . is not considered for totalCharacters

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description name type optional desc ... Any Yes Remaining arguments.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Provides a text based date based on input given.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Integer Yes Only accepts the following values: 0, 1, and 2

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text:
Depending on the number passed to the function, will get the following String returned:

Value Result Example 0 (Default if empty) Full text date January 1, 2026 1 ISO Standard Date Format 2026-01-01 2 Filename safe output 2026_01_01

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Yup!

import foo

def bar():
    continue
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    bar()
